metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the generated email includes all required key facts accurately and naturally.

rubric.description: The email ignores the required facts or contradicts them.
Only a tiny fragment of one required fact appears.
Some facts are present, but multiple important facts are missing or distorted.
Most facts are present, but one important fact is missing, weakened, or ambiguous.
Almost all required facts are present, with only a minor omission or awkward compression.
Every required fact is included accurately and naturally, with no unsupported factual claims.

metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the generated email matches the requested tone in context.

rubric.description: The tone is absent, opposite of the requested tone, or unusable for the context.
The tone is very far from the requested tone.
The tone only loosely resembles the requested tone and is noticeably mismatched.
The tone is partly aligned but generic, uneven, or inconsistent.
The tone is mostly aligned with only small mismatches.
The tone closely and consistently matches the requested tone throughout.

metric_definitions.definition: Measures whether the output works as a professional email.

rubric.description: The output is not usable as an email.
It barely resembles a professional email or collapses greeting, body, closing, and signature into a flat single paragraph.
Some email elements are present, but structure is incomplete, hard to scan, or mostly unformatted.
Basic email content is present, but paragraphing, greeting, body, or sign-off formatting is noticeably weak.
The email is clear and professional with only minor structure or formatting rough edges.
The subject and body are polished, clear, concise, and formatted with distinct greeting, body paragraphing, and sign-off.

metrics.reason: The email includes all required facts, including the date, time, the list of attendees, and the specific request for waiting instructions, all presented accurately and professionally.
The email is concise, polite, and professional. It clearly communicates the purpose of the message, includes all necessary details, and maintains a respectful tone throughout.

The email contains all necessary information and maintains a professional tone, but it is formatted as a single block of text without line breaks between the greeting, body, and sign-off, which negatively impacts readability and professional structure.

The email includes all required facts: the meeting is for next week, lists all three specific time slots, asks for availability, and mentions the need to finalize the schedule soon. The tone is professional and concise.

The email is professional, concise, and collaborative. It clearly communicates the purpose, lists all required time slots, and invites team input while maintaining a polite and efficient tone throughout.

The email contains all necessary information and maintains a professional tone, but it is presented as a single block of text without line breaks between the greeting, body, and sign-off, which hinders readability and professional formatting.

The email includes all required facts: invoice number 48217, the due date of April 30, 2026, the outstanding amount of $3,450, and a request for an update on the payment date. The tone is professional and firm, and all sender details are correctly included.

The email perfectly balances a firm tone regarding the overdue payment with a polite and professional delivery. It clearly states all necessary facts, including the invoice number, due date, and amount, while maintaining a respectful and business-appropriate demeanor throughout.

The email contains all necessary information but collapses the greeting, body, and sign-off into a single paragraph, which makes it difficult to read and fails to follow standard professional email formatting.
The email includes every required fact accurately and naturally, while maintaining the requested professional, apologetic, and concise tone.
The email includes all required facts, maintains a professional and courteous tone, expresses a sincere apology for the inconvenience, and is concise, perfectly aligning with the requested tone.
The email contains all the required information, but it is presented as a single, flat paragraph, which collapses the greeting, body, and sign-off into one block of text, making it difficult to scan.

The email accurately includes all required facts, including the conference dates, the early-bird deadline, both registration fees, and the encouragement to register early, all while maintaining the requested tone.

The email perfectly balances an informative, upbeat, and professional tone. It clearly presents all required facts while maintaining a polite and encouraging demeanor that aligns with the requested tone.
The email is well-structured, professional, and clear. It includes all necessary information, uses appropriate greeting and sign-off, and maintains a positive, informative tone.

The email accurately includes all three required facts: the new design review completion date of Friday, 19 June, 2026, the reason for the delay being missing stakeholder feedback, and the confirmation that the final launch date remains unchanged. The tone is professional, calm, and reassuring.

The email is transparent about the delay and its cause, maintains a calm and professional tone, and provides clear reassurance regarding the final launch date, perfectly aligning with the requested tone.
The email contains all necessary information and maintains a professional tone, but it is formatted as a single paragraph, making it difficult to scan and lacking proper professional structure.

The email includes all required facts: it mentions the Q3 roadmap priorities, notes the attached summary of decisions and action items, and clearly requests corrections by the end of the day Thursday.
The email is clear, concise, and maintains a professional tone throughout. It effectively communicates the purpose and the deadline, aligning perfectly with the requested tone and intent.

The email contains all necessary information and maintains a professional tone, but it is presented as a single block of text without proper line breaks between the greeting, body, and sign-off, which hinders readability.
The email includes all required facts, including the date and time, the equipment requirements, and the provision of lunch and water, while maintaining the requested friendly and appreciative tone.
The email consistently maintains a friendly, encouraging, and appreciative tone throughout, effectively inviting volunteers while expressing genuine gratitude for their contributions.
The email contains all the necessary information and maintains a professional tone, but it lacks proper paragraphing, resulting in a single block of text that is difficult to scan.
The email includes all three required facts accurately and naturally, and maintains the requested formal and informative tone.
The email maintains a professional and formal tone throughout, clearly communicating all required policy updates without unnecessary informality.

The email contains all necessary information, but the formatting is weak because the greeting, body, and sign-off are collapsed into a single paragraph rather than using distinct line breaks for professional readability.

The email accurately includes all required facts: the maintenance window of Sunday from 1:00 AM to 3:00 AM, the potential portal unavailability, and the expectation that services will resume immediately after. The tone is professional and brief.
The email is concise, professional, and clearly communicates the maintenance window and expected service resumption, perfectly matching the requested tone.

The email contains all necessary information, but it lacks proper line breaks between the greeting, body, and sign-off, causing it to appear as a single paragraph, which hinders readability and professional formatting.
